records.ety: [OE. entaile carving, OF. entaille, F., an incision, fr. entailler to cut away; pref. en- (L. in) + tailler to cut; LL. feudum talliatum a fee entailed, i. e., curtailed or limited. See Tail limitation, Tailor.]

defs: An estate in fee entailed, or limited in descent to a particular class of issue.
The rule by which the descent is fixed.
quotes: A power of breaking the ancient entails, and of alienating their estates.

records.ety: [OE. entailen to carve, OF. entailler. See Entail, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Entailed; p. pr. & vb. n. Entailing.]

defs: To settle or fix inalienably on a person or thing, or on a person and his descendants or a certain line of descendants; -- said especially of an estate; to bestow as an heritage.
quotes: Allowing them to entail their estates.
I here entail The crown to thee and to thine heirs forever.
To entail him and his heirs unto the crown.

defs: To cut or carve in an ornamental way.
